hypothesis: If the AMDEN diffusion model with an EGNN backbone is trained on amorphous silicon datasets with different thermal histories and on a multi-element glass (MEG) dataset, and the MEG model is conditioned on Young's modulus via classifier-free guidance, then the generated amorphous structures will reproduce the reference RDFs, bond-angle distributions, structure factors, and potential energy distributions, and MEG structures will be steerable to the target modulus, because the denoising SDE framework, optional energy-based refinement with HMC, and density-control mechanisms encode and realize the physical structure–property relationships present in the training data.
